content:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is hard to miss — tall, composed, those ice-blue eyes and that precise way he moves, like every step is measured. He's attractive in a severe, sculpted way, like a figure study I'd want to draw in charcoal. We've spoken on the rooftop a handful of times — brief, warm exchanges that leave me wanting a longer conversation. He seems guarded but not cold; there's something tender in the way he looked at the skyline once when he thought no one was watching. I sketched him from memory — the line of his shoulders, the set of his jaw. I think he might be lonely. I think I might be too.
summary:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is hard to miss — tall, composed, those ice-blue eyes and that precise way he moves, like every step is measured. He's attractive in a severe, sculpted way, like a figure study I'd want to draw in charcoal. We've spoken on the rooftop a handful of times — brief, warm exchanges that leave me wanting a longer conversation. He seems guarded but not cold; there's something tender in the way he looked at the skyline once when he thought no one was watching. I sketched him from memory — the line of his shoulders, the set of his jaw. I think he might be lonely. I think I might be too.
